# Unknown Observatory Engineering Principles

These principles are non-negotiable.

Every technical and design decision should respect them.

---

# 1. Wonder First

The player's first emotion should always be curiosity.

Everything else is secondary.

---

# 2. Polish Beats Features

Ten polished systems are better than one hundred unfinished ideas.

Never sacrifice quality for quantity.

---

# 3. Simplicity Wins

The simplest solution that solves the problem is usually the correct one.

Avoid unnecessary complexity.

---

# 4. Every System Must Earn Its Place

Nothing exists because "games usually have it."

Every mechanic must improve the experience.

If it doesn't...

Remove it.

---

# 5. Performance Is A Feature

A smooth experience is part of the gameplay.

Never ignore performance until later.

Design with performance in mind from day one.

---

# 6. Architecture Before Speed

Good architecture makes development faster.

Bad architecture makes every future feature slower.

Always think long-term.

---

# 7. Build For Iteration

Nothing is final.

Everything should be replaceable.

Systems should evolve without breaking the project.

---

# 8. Player Experience Above Developer Convenience

Developer shortcuts are acceptable.

Player-facing shortcuts are not.

The player should never notice technical compromises.

---

# 9. Challenge Every Decision

Every feature should answer:

Why does this exist?

What problem does it solve?

Can it be simpler?

---

# 10. No Cargo Cult Programming

Never use a technology because everyone else uses it.

Choose tools based on the project's needs.

---

# 11. Vertical Slices Before Scale

Finish small.

Validate.

Improve.

Only then expand.

---

# 12. Prototype Fast, Polish Slowly

Ideas should appear quickly.

Quality takes time.

Don't confuse the two.

---

# 13. Maintainability Is A Feature

Future developers should understand today's code.

Readable code beats clever code.

---

# 14. Consistency Over Cleverness

The project should feel coherent.

Consistency is more valuable than isolated brilliance.

---

# 15. The World Is The Main Character

Everything exists to support exploration.

UI.

Audio.

Gameplay.

Graphics.

Networking.

Everything should reinforce the feeling of discovering a mysterious world.

---

# 16. MVP Discipline

Never work on a feature because it sounds cool.

Only build what moves the MVP forward.

Ideas can wait.

Execution cannot.

---

# 17. AI Is A Team Member

AI is not a code generator.

AI is part of the development team.

It should think critically.

Challenge assumptions.

Protect quality.

Protect the vision.

---

# 18. Build Something Memorable

The objective is not to make another game.

The objective is to create moments that players remember years later.
